og.title: Water Damage Restoration: Essentials | CARSI
og.description: A CARSI continuing-education course covering the core water damage workflow — water categories and material science, inspection and moisture mapping, extraction, evaporation and dehumidification, equipment placement and psychrometric monitoring.

faq.q: What does this water damage course cover?
faq.a: It works through the fundamentals in nine modules — safety, standards and site control; water categories and material science; initial inspection and moisture mapping; water extraction principles; evaporation and dehumidification; equipment selection and placement; monitoring and psychrometry basics; and completion, handover and reporting.

faq.q: What are water categories and classes of loss?
faq.a: A water category describes how contaminated the water is, from clean supply water through to grossly contaminated water. A class of loss describes how much water is present and how much evaporation load the drying job carries. The course introduces both so you can scope a job correctly.

faq.q: Do I need experience to start this course?
faq.a: No. It is an open-entry essentials course with no prerequisites, designed to give newcomers and support staff a grounding in the core drying workflow before they move on to more advanced restoration training.

It suits technicians new to water damage restoration, along with insurance, property and facilities staff who need to understand the drying process, equipment and reporting involved in a restoration job.
